raw_output:
PR #1 correctness review, rounds 1/3/4 (Claude, Fable 5, independent fresh-context passes).

ROUND 4 (final, head ca27180), verbatim summary: F1 (P2) test_hash_sees_submodule_pointer_change_despite_ignore_all became vacuous after the merge-base binding (different base refs differ via merge-base id alone; empirically verified the pin's removal no longer fails the test). F2 (P3) final assertion of test_hash_of_non_empty_diff trivially true for the same reason. F3 (nit) rule 4 / docstring state "every base advance restales" unconditionally; true only after a branch update (require-up-to-date dependency documented elsewhere in the same file). F4 (nit) diff.indentHeuristic unpinned (fail-closed spurious STALE only). F5 (nit) git merge-base failure with empty stderr prints a blank diagnostic. Overall: merge-base binding implemented exactly as documented (id + NUL + raw diff bytes); --submodule=short valid; restale regression test genuinely proves the bypass precondition and closure; CI/doc/version consistency holds; suite 44/44.

ROUND 3 (head 77c4024): F1 (P2) docs overclaimed that base advance + branch update always restales artifacts — empirically false for disjoint base advances (same defect codex security reported as high; fixed by binding the merge-base id into the hash). F2 (P3) diff.submodule unpinned — user config flips the hash fail-closed on gitlink diffs. F3 (nit) doc pin list missing suppressBlankEmpty. F4 (nit) v0.1 vs v0.1.0 changelog inconsistency.

ROUND 1 (head bd4b47a): F1 (P2) non-string reviewed_diff_sha256 crashes gate with traceback instead of diagnostic (reproduced with 4 payload types). F2 (P3) "pins every byte-affecting knob" claim false: srcPrefix/dstPrefix/orderFile/interHunkContext/suppressBlankEmpty unpinned (verified two flipped the hash). F3 (P3) wave plan misdescribed SKILL.md/rationale.md as pre-existing; both new in git. F4 (P3) "reason": null satisfied the reason requirement via str() coercion. F5 (nit) test named "medium" used P2 on a review leg. F6 (nit) outside-a-git-repo import dies with opaque CalledProcessError. F7 (nit) process doc wrote the staleness command as git diff <merge-base>...HEAD. Overall round 1: fail-closed in every path probed; no wrongful-pass defect.

DELTA VERIFICATION (post round-1 fixes, Claude Fable 5): confirmed F1, F3-F7, S1-S3 correctly closed with genuine regression tests; found NEW P1: the F2 fix's -c diff.orderFile= (empty) makes git fatal on any non-empty diff — gate unusable end-to-end (fail-closed). Verified /dev/null variant deterministic. Fixed as b511d76 with non-empty-diff regression test.

--- Post-review delta note (orchestrator, attested at final head 28f1fdc) ---
After the round-4 legs ran on ca27180, their own validated findings were fixed
and committed as 28f1fdc: the vacuous submodule/non-empty-diff hash tests were
rebuilt around a constant merge-base (mutation-verified: the submodule test
fails when --ignore-submodules=none is removed), diff.indentHeuristic pinned,
merge-base failure diagnostic given an unrelated-histories fallback, docstring
and rule-4 wording softened to name the require-up-to-date ruleset dependency,
doc pin list synced. 44/44 tests pass; git diff --check clean. The attested
hash covers the fixed state. Second delta (49974df, attested hash updated):
the round-4 watch-item flake became a hard CI failure — porcelain `git add
<submodule>` under ignore=all staged nothing on CI's git version. The test now
stages the gitlink via update-index --cacheinfo (plumbing, immune to ignore
config) and surfaces git stderr per the round-4 recommendation; mutation check
re-verified (test fails without --ignore-submodules=none), 5x stable, 44/44.
Test-infrastructure-only change; gate logic untouched. Battery history: round 1 (Claude legs) -> fixes;
delta verification caught a P1 the fixes introduced (orderFile crash) -> fixed;
round 2/3 (codex legs + Claude re-review) found the submodule ignore=all hash
bypass (high, reproduced) and the merge-base binding gap (high/P2, found
independently by both models) -> fixed; round 4 (all four legs) -> no new
validated blocking findings: converged.

findings.summary: Non-string reviewed_diff_sha256 crashes gate with traceback, not diagnostic
findings.reason: Type guard + parametrized regression test (5 payload types).

findings.summary: Diff-hash byte-stability claim false: several git config knobs unpinned
Wave plan misdescribed SKILL.md/rationale.md as pre-existing files
findings.reason: Plan now records both as new-in-git, dropped in pre-wave.

findings.summary: reason: null satisfied the dismissal-reason requirement via str() coercion
Test named 'medium' used P2 on a review leg
Running outside a git repo dies with opaque CalledProcessError at import
Process doc wrote staleness command as git diff <merge-base>...HEAD
findings.reason: Doc now shows git diff origin/main...HEAD.

findings.summary: Round-1 fix introduced -c diff.orderFile= (empty) — git fatals on any non-empty diff, gate unusable
findings.reason: Found by delta verification; /dev/null pin, stderr surfaced as diagnostic, non-empty-diff regression test; verified deterministic.

findings.summary: Docs overclaimed that base advance + branch update always restales artifacts
findings.reason: Same defect as codex R3-Z1; fixed by binding merge-base id into the hash, making the documented claim true.

findings.summary: diff.submodule config unpinned — flips hash fail-closed on gitlink diffs
Merge-base binding made the submodule regression test vacuous
findings.reason: Rebuilt as two branches off one base (constant merge-base); mutation-verified the test fails when the pin is removed. Also found by codex R4.

findings.summary: Final assertion of test_hash_of_non_empty_diff trivially true after binding
'Every base advance restales' stated unconditionally in rule 4 and docstring
findings.reason: Wording names the require-up-to-date ruleset dependency.

findings.summary: merge-base failure with empty stderr prints blank diagnostic
